articles.title: Watsco Declares $3.30 Quarterly Dividend
articles.summary: Watsco, Inc. (NYSE: WSO) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $3.30 per share on its Common and Class B common stock, payable on April 30, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 16, 2026. This dividend reflects a 10% increase, bringing the annual rate to $13.20 per share, and marks 52 consecutive years of dividend payments. The company's Chairman & CEO, Albert H. Nahmad, noted that this increase demonstrates confidence in the business and its strong balance sheet.

articles.title: Watsco Declares $3.30 Quarterly Dividend

articles.title: Watsco (WSO) slides as investors refocus on soft early-2026 HVAC demand signals
articles.summary: Watsco (WSO) stock is down 5.4% today, likely due to investor concerns over slowing HVAC/R demand following the company's Q4 and full-year 2025 update. Management indicated mid-single-digit sales declines in January and February 2026, reinforcing worries about near-term volume trends ahead of the spring/summer selling season. The decline may also reflect profit-taking and broader market de-risking in cyclical stocks.

articles.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Encourages Watsco, Inc. (WSO) Investors to Inquire about Securities Investigation
articles.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential securities claims against Watsco, Inc. (WSO) following a 2% year-over-year revenue decline in Q1 2025, which missed consensus estimates and led to an 11% stock drop. The firm encourages investors who purchased Watsco securities and are aware of relevant facts to contact them to assist with the investigation, which operates on a contingency fee basis.

articles.title: Dividend Hike Amid Softer Earnings Might Change The Case For Investing In Watsco (WSO)
articles.summary: Watsco (NYSE: WSO) recently announced a 10% dividend increase to US$13.20 per share, despite reporting softer sales and net income for Q4 2025. This move highlights management's focus on shareholder returns and improved gross margins, even as the company navigates the disruptive shift to A2L refrigerant equipment. Investors are encouraged to assess how this dividend hike, alongside a debt-free balance sheet, redefines the investment narrative amidst near-term industry challenges and tariff uncertainty.

articles.title: Dividend raised as Watsco (NYSE: WSO) posts 2025 results and record margin
articles.summary: Watsco, Inc. reported mixed financial results for 2025, with revenues down 5% to $7.24 billion and diluted EPS at $12.25, reflecting a challenging HVAC/R market. Despite this, the company achieved a record gross margin of 28.0% and strong operating cash flow of $570 million, ending the year debt-free with $780 million in cash. In response, Watsco's board approved a 10% increase in its annual dividend to $13.20 per share, marking its 52nd consecutive year of dividend payments, underscoring confidence in long-term cash generation and strategic investments in digital platforms.

articles.title: Watsco Boosts Annual Dividend 10% to $13.20 Per Share
articles.summary: Watsco, Inc. announced a 10% increase in its annual dividend to $13.20 per share, reflecting confidence in its business prospects, strong cash flow, and a conservative balance sheet. This marks the 52nd consecutive year the company has paid dividends, emphasizing its strategy to share cash flow while growing its distribution network and leveraging technology. The increase will be reflected in the next quarterly dividend payment in April 2026.

articles.title: Watsco Boosts Annual Dividend 10% to $13.20 Per Share
